label: Lover / Handler
description: Katje is deployed to seduce and monitor Slothrop as part of the intelligence apparatus's investigation into his rocket-sexual map.

label: Manipulator → Subject
description: Pointsman orchestrates the surveillance and conditioning experiments around Slothrop, seeking to prove Pavlovian causality.

label: Master / Captive
description: Blicero keeps Gottfried as his sexual captive and ultimately fires him inside Rocket 00000 as the novel's terminal act of domination.

label: Colonial Master / Former Captive
description: In colonial South-West Africa, Blicero (then Weissmann) took the young Enzian as his sexual captive. This relationship shapes both men's trajectories through the novel.

label: Half-Brothers / Adversaries
description: Half-siblings who have never met. Tchitcherine is driven to find and kill Enzian, though the novel suggests this quest may be futile.

label: Counterforce Allies
description: Pirate and Katje both become aligned with the Counterforce resistance against They.

label: Counterforce Allies
description: Roger Mexico and Pirate Prentice both become key figures in the Counterforce.

label: Historical Conditioning
description: Jamf allegedly conditioned the infant Slothrop using Imipolex G, creating the mysterious stimulus-response pattern that drives the plot.

label: Wartime Lovers
description: The novel's most tender relationship, existing in the temporary democracy of the Blitz and threatened by peacetime's return to order.

label: Lovers
description: Geli Tripping becomes Tchitcherine's lover in the Zone, eventually using her witchcraft to save him from his destructive quest.

label: Ideological Opponents
description: Pointsman (cause-and-effect Pavlovian) vs Roger Mexico (probability and randomness) — the novel's central epistemological conflict.
